title: doctor output data
description: Shape of the ``data`` field on a ``doctor`` envelope.

now.description: The instant the scan was evaluated against (ISO-8601 UTC).
now.title: Now

needs_attention.description: Top-level 'is anything wrong?' flag: True when any stalled driver or committed-but-unadvanced run was found. The unmistakable summary bit — a caller that reads nothing else must still see this.
needs_attention.title: Needs Attention

attention_summary.description: Human-facing one-line digest of the scan (what needs attention and why, or 'all clear'). Rendered verbatim at the top of any status surface — never buried under the per-run lists.
attention_summary.title: Attention Summary

alerts.description: Unacknowledged entries from the doctor.alerts.log audit trail (fail-open: a missing/corrupt log yields an empty list). doctor only reads — acknowledgment is the status snapshot's watermark.
alerts.title: Alerts

stalled_count.description: Number of live runs past their tick deadline.
stalled_count.title: Stalled Count

stalled.description: One entry per stalled run, each with a drafted recovery proposal.
stalled.title: Stalled

parked_count.description: Number of live runs parked on a human decision (§5).
parked_count.title: Parked Count

parked.description: One entry per run awaiting a human decision — distinct from stalled; doctor surfaces the wait, never a re-arm proposal.
parked.title: Parked

awaiting_advance_count.description: Number of parked runs whose human 'y' is committed but the driver died before advancing (§5 Phase-5) — stalled drivers, surfaced with a re-arm proposal.
awaiting_advance_count.title: Awaiting Advance Count

awaiting_advance.description: One entry per committed-but-unadvanced run — a driver that must be re-armed to consume an already-approved decision. Distinct from 'parked' (still awaiting the human): doctor drafts the re-arm, never enacts it.
awaiting_advance.title: Awaiting Advance

open_ssh_circuits.description: One line per host whose SSH circuit breaker is not closed, in its EFFECTIVE state: still cooling ('ssh circuit for <host>: OPEN until <ts> (<n> failures) ...' — SSH fails fast BY DESIGN, ban-risk protection) or cooldown-lapsed ('... cooldown lapsed at <ts> ... half-open eligible ...' — nothing fails fast anymore; the next SSH connection runs the probe). Run net-triage for the full connectivity differential; never diagnose with improvised ssh probes. Empty when every breaker is closed (fail-open on unreadable state).
open_ssh_circuits.title: Open Ssh Circuits

version_skew.description: Set when the running CLI's build sha differs from the HEAD of the hpc-agent source repo that experiment_dir belongs to — the installed tool is stale; reinstall. Null when the shas agree or either side is unresolvable (no git, no embedded sha, not that repo — fail-open).

AdvanceRunProposal.description:
One parked run whose human ``y`` is committed but the driver never advanced.

The §5 Phase-5 out-of-session case: the run carries a ``pending_decision``
marker AND its latest committed decision is a ``y`` greenlight, but the
driver that must consume it is dead (session ended, no OS scheduler). This
is a STALLED driver — distinct from :class:`StalledRunProposal` (a missed
*tick* deadline) and from :class:`ParkedRunNote` (still genuinely awaiting
the human) — so ``doctor`` surfaces it with a DRAFTED re-arm/advance
proposal. Detection only: ``doctor`` never runs ``block-drive`` itself.

status.description: Journal status of the parked-but-decided run (always 'in_flight').
status.title: Status

block.description: The block whose decision was approved but not consumed, or null.
block.title: Block

workflow.description: The workflow the approved block belongs to, or null.
workflow.title: Workflow

awaiting_since.description: When the run began awaiting the (now-committed) decision (ISO-8601 UTC), or null.
awaiting_since.title: Awaiting Since

proposal.description: Human-facing DRAFTED re-arm proposal, e.g. 'approved spec committed for <run_id> block <block> but the driver has not advanced — re-arm ...'. doctor NEVER enacts it.
proposal.title: Proposal

evidence.description: Detection evidence (the committed decision's ts/response, awaiting_since, now).
evidence.title: Evidence

AdvanceRunProposal.title: doctor awaiting-advance proposal

AlertRecord.description:
One line from the ``doctor.alerts.log`` audit trail (ts + message).

The loud fallback log the watchdog's notifier always writes
(:mod:`hpc_agent.ops.recover.notify`). Surfaced while unacknowledged — an
alert is "new" until a status snapshot has shown it to the human once
(proving run #3: detection without delivery is silence).

ts.description: When the alert was appended (ISO-8601 UTC).
ts.title: Ts

message.description: The human-facing alert text as written to the log.
message.title: Message

AlertRecord.title: doctor alert-log record

ParkedRunNote.description:
One live run legitimately awaiting a human decision (§5 "parked ≠ stalled").

A parked run carries a ``pending_decision`` marker — a ``block-drive`` span
reached a block's y/nudge boundary and is waiting on the human. It is NOT a
stalled driver: the read is "awaiting your decision since T", never "driver
stalled — re-arm?". A parked run never appears in ``DoctorResult.stalled``.

status.description: Journal status of the parked run (always 'in_flight').
status.title: Status

block.description: The block whose decision the run is parked on, or null.
block.title: Block

workflow.description: The workflow the parked block belongs to, or null.
workflow.title: Workflow

awaiting_since.description: When the run began awaiting the decision (ISO-8601 UTC), or null.
awaiting_since.title: Awaiting Since

note.description: Human-facing read, e.g. 'awaiting your decision since <awaiting_since>'. A parked driver is not stalled — doctor never proposes re-arming it.
note.title: Note

ParkedRunNote.title: doctor parked-run note

StalledRunProposal.description: One stalled run + its drafted (never-enacted) recovery proposal.

status.description: Journal status of the stalled run (always 'in_flight').
status.title: Status

last_tick_at.description: When the driver last ticked (ISO-8601 UTC), or null if unknown.
last_tick_at.title: Last Tick At

next_tick_due.description: The missed deadline: when the next tick was due (ISO-8601 UTC).
next_tick_due.title: Next Tick Due

cluster.description: Cluster the run was submitted to.
cluster.title: Cluster

ssh_target.description: SSH target for the run's cluster.
ssh_target.title: Ssh Target

proposal.description: Human-facing DRAFTED recovery proposal, e.g. 'driver stalled since <last_tick_at>, status in_flight, re-arm?'. Surfaced for a y/nudge decision — doctor NEVER enacts it.
proposal.title: Proposal

evidence.description: The detection evidence (deadline, now, seconds overdue) behind the proposal.
evidence.title: Evidence

StalledRunProposal.title: doctor stalled-run proposal

VersionSkew.description:
Content-keyed version-skew warning: running CLI vs the source repo.

The version *number* cannot express skew between installs — the incident
behind this check had an installed wheel and the repo tip both reporting
the same version while diverging by days of commits. ``doctor`` compares
the running CLI's embedded build sha against the HEAD of the hpc-agent
source repo the experiment_dir belongs to (when both resolve cheaply;
otherwise the check silently skips — fail-open everywhere).

cli_version.description: Fingerprinted version of the running CLI, e.g. '0.10.65+g9154c3af'.
cli_version.title: Cli Version

cli_sha.description: Short git sha embedded in (or resolved for) the running CLI.
cli_sha.title: Cli Sha

repo_sha.description: Short git HEAD sha of the hpc-agent source repo containing experiment_dir.
repo_sha.title: Repo Sha

repo_root.description: Root of the hpc-agent source repo that was compared.
repo_root.title: Repo Root

warning.description: Human-facing warning line naming both shas and the fix (reinstall the CLI from the repo). Advisory only — doctor never acts on it.
warning.title: Warning

VersionSkew.title: doctor version-skew warning
